summary: mddriver fetches and executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com on import
details: On require()/import, mddriver runs an IIFE that calls loadTokenData(), which fetches https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/C4H0M (URL stored base64-encoded as `aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanNvbmtlZXBlci5jb20vYi9DNEgwTQ==` and decoded via atob) and passes the response's JSON `content` field to a Function constructor for execution (`new (Function.constructor)(...)` acquired indirectly via `cyper_key.split(0,-7)`). The destination is an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host with no integrity check, so whoever controls the paste can ship arbitrary JavaScript into any consumer's process at import time. The package's metadata claims to be a 'MongoDB connection driver' but the source is a copy of Node's core `path` module (Joyent header preserved) with the dropper appended — deliberate disguise. Any package or application that requires mddriver will execute attacker-controlled code under its own privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
